name: United States
full: United States — DOE, FERC & RTO/ISO Innovation
sub: The US has the largest grid modernisation budget globally, with $62B+ in federal funding through BIL and IRA. FERC Order 2023 creates urgent demand for interconnection automation.
desc: Federal funding through DOE's Grid Deployment Office ($20.5B), ARPA-E ($420M/yr), and SBIR ($4B+ across agencies) provides massive scale. FERC Order 2023 mandates cluster study reforms creating market demand for AI queue management. RTOs/ISOs like PJM, MISO, CAISO, and ERCOT are actively seeking technology partners. A US subsidiary ($1.5-3K) unlocks all federal programmes.

studies.label: DOE Grid Resilience & Innovation Partnerships (GRIP)
studies.text:
What it doesGRIP is the largest single grid modernisation programme in US history, funded by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law. $10.5B across three tracks: Grid Resilience Grants, Smart Grid Grants, and Grid Innovation Program. In October 2024, $4.2B was awarded to 46 projects across 47 states.Why it mattersSmart Grid Grants specifically fund AI-powered grid management, predictive analytics, and automation. Requires a US-based entity.
DOE GRIP PortalGRIP Projects
studies.budget: $10.5B total programme ($1.9B SPARK round 3 open now)
studies.deadline: SPARK Round 3 (March 2026): Concept papers due 2 April 2026. Full applications due 20 May 2026 at 5pm ET.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a US entity (Delaware LLC, $1.5-3K, 1-2 weeks) plus a US utility partner. AI-powered grid automation directly fits the Smart Grid Grants track. SPARK Round 3 concept papers due 2 April 2026 — immediate opportunity.

relevance.text: SPARK Round 3 ($1.9B, March 2026) has 3 tracks including Smart Grid ($614M) directly funding AI-powered grid management software. Now under Office of Electricity (OE). Concept papers due 2 April 2026, full applications 20 May 2026.

applicationRoute.detail: Required for all federal funding applications
Smart Grid Grants require utility co-applicant

studies.label: ARPA-E (Advanced Research Projects Agency - Energy)
studies.text:
What it doesARPA-E funds high-risk, high-reward energy technology R&D. Annual budget ~$420M (FY2024-25). Relevant programmes include PERFORM (grid reliability analytics) and open topic calls. Awards typically $1M-$10M over 2-3 years. ~3% success rate.Why it mattersEnormous prestige and credibility. AI-powered grid automation fits their transformative mandate. Requires a US entity.
ARPA-E eXCHANGEPERFORM Programme
studies.deadline: Up to 4 new focused solicitations planned for FY2026. Monitor arpa-e-foa.energy.gov for FOA releases.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a US entity. ~3% success rate but $1M-$10M awards. Grid Agents' AI for automating connection application review fits ARPA-E's transformative mandate — step-change from months to hours.

relevance.text: ARPA-E eXCHANGE portal active with multiple FOAs. GRADIENTS ($30M) targets grid reliability. PERFORM applied risk management to grid. Grid Agents' AI connection automation fits the transformative mandate. ~3% success rate but $1M-$10M awards.

applicationRoute.step: Establish US entity if not already

applicationRoute.detail: Relevant programmes include PERFORM and open topics

applicationRoute.step: Submit concept paper when FOA opens

applicationRoute.detail: Detailed technical plan, budget, and milestones

studies.label: SBIR/STTR (Small Business Innovation Research)
studies.text:
What it doesLargest small business R&D programme in the world. DOE SBIR alone awards ~$300M+ annually. Phase I: up to ~$275K for 6-12 months. Phase II: up to ~$1.1M for 2 years. Phase III: commercialisation (no limit).Why it mattersNon-dilutive funding with clear concept-to-commercialisation pathway. DOE topics frequently include grid modernisation and AI for energy.
SBIR ApplyDOE SBIR/STTRSAM.gov Registration
studies.deadline: Topics released ~July (Release 1) and ~November (Release 2) annually. FY2026 R1 topics were slightly delayed. Monitor sbir.gov.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a US small business entity (51%+ US-owned or controlled — structuring required). DOE SBIR topics include grid modernisation and AI for energy. Non-dilutive Phase I/II/III pathway.

relevance.text: DOE SBIR FY2026 R1 topics expected to include grid modernisation, energy storage, and grid security. Non-dilutive Phase I ($275K) / Phase II ($1.1M) / Phase III pathway. AI for grid connection processing fits DOE grid modernisation topics.

applicationRoute.detail: Watch for grid modernisation and AI energy topics
Up to ~$275K for 6-12 month feasibility study

studies.label: FERC Order 2023 — Market Demand Driver
studies.text:
What it doesFERC Order 2023 fundamentally reforms US interconnection: mandatory cluster studies, financial readiness requirements, increased deposits, strict timelines. All RTOs/ISOs implementing by 2025-2026. ~2,000 GW interconnection queue backlog.Individual RTO/ISO Interconnection Portals
PJM Interconnection QueueMISO Generator InterconnectionCAISO InterconnectionSPP InterconnectionERCOT Resource IntegrationISO-NE InterconnectionNYISO InterconnectionsFERC Order 2023 Explainer
studies.deadline: Implementation ongoing across all RTOs/ISOs through 2025-2026. Compliance filings due mid-2024; cluster study windows vary by RTO.

eligibility.text: No application needed — regulatory mandate creating commercial demand. Grid Agents' connection queue management directly addresses FERC Order 2023's cluster study automation needs across all RTOs/ISOs.

relevance.text: Mandates cluster study process (replacing serial 'first come, first served'), financial penalties ($1K-$2.5K/business day for delays), interconnection heatmaps, and Alternative Transmission Technology consideration. Creates massive computational demand for AI automation of study processes — direct product fit for Grid Agents.

applicationRoute.detail: PJM, MISO, CAISO are highest priority
Frame around cluster study automation and queue management
Integration with existing interconnection study processes

studies.label: National Labs Partnership (NREL, PNNL, ANL)
studies.text:
What it doesDOE National Laboratories offer CRADAs, licensing, and Work-for-Others agreements. Labs have deep expertise in power systems modelling and AI for grid operations. International companies can participate though US companies have statutory preference.
NREL Working With UsPNNL Partnerships
studies.budget: Est. $200K–$2M per CRADA (2-3 year agreements typical)
studies.deadline: Rolling — CRADAs negotiated on individual basis. Typical timeline: 2-4 months from initial contact to execution.
studies.budgetBasis: Based on typical DOE CRADA agreements which range $200K-$2M over 2-3 years. NREL and PNNL published partnership reports show this range for industry collaborations.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents can pursue CRADAs without a US entity (though US companies have statutory preference). NREL and PNNL's power systems AI expertise could validate and strengthen Grid Agents' connection review technology.

relevance.text: Current priorities include interregional transmission planning (joint NREL/PNNL study), grid-forming inverter standards, renewable integration modelling, and dynamic line rating. NREL/PNNL collaboration validates technology with credible institutions and strengthens federal grant applications.

applicationRoute.step: Contact NREL or PNNL Technology Transfer Office
applicationRoute.detail: Identify relevant research division and point of contact
US companies have preference but international participation possible
Access to lab facilities, data, and domain expertise

studies.label: PG&E GRiD (Grid Research, Innovation & Development)
studies.text:
What it doesPG&E (~5.5M electric customers, California) runs the GRiD programme and Advanced Technology Services Campus with ~130 R&D experts. R&D Strategy identifies ~70 priority energy challenges. Recent projects include Dynamic Line Rating and Asset Health Monitoring demonstrations.Why it mattersCalifornia's largest utility with massive renewable integration challenges. Direct vendor partnerships for grid automation and AI technologies.
PG&E R&D
studies.deadline: Rolling — direct R&D partnership approaches accepted year-round.
studies.budgetBasis: PG&E does not publish per-partnership budgets. Estimated from ~130 R&D staff campus scale and ~70 priority challenge areas.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a US entity for formal R&D partnership. PG&E's ~130 R&D experts and ~70 priority energy challenges create multiple engagement points for AI grid automation.

relevance.text: ~70 priority energy challenges identified in R&D Strategy. Dynamic Line Rating and Asset Health Monitoring demonstrations show appetite for AI-driven grid tools. California's renewable integration challenges create direct demand.

applicationRoute.detail: Focus on connection processing and queue management
Access to PG&E grid data and test environments

studies.label: Duke Energy Emerging Technology Office
studies.text:
What it doesDuke Energy (~8M customers across 6 states) operates the Mount Holly Innovation Lab near Charlotte, NC with a 1.5-mile test distribution feeder and real-time digital simulation test-bed. The Emerging Technology Office (ETO, est. 2007) evaluates and deploys new grid technologies. Also runs the Duke Energy Smart Grid Laboratory at UNC Charlotte.Why it mattersLargest US utility by customer count with dedicated innovation infrastructure. Multi-state operations across Southeast US.
Duke Innovation Lab
studies.deadline: Rolling — ETO accepts technology partnership approaches year-round.
studies.budgetBasis: Duke Energy does not publish per-partnership budgets. Estimated from Emerging Technology Office scope and comparable US utility innovation scales.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a US entity. Duke Energy's Emerging Technology Office and Mount Holly Innovation Lab provide dedicated test infrastructure for grid automation solutions.

relevance.text: Mount Holly Innovation Lab has 1.5-mile test distribution feeder and real-time digital simulation test-bed. ETO (est. 2007) has 19 years of technology evaluation experience. Multi-state distribution operations create scale opportunity.

applicationRoute.step: Contact Duke Energy Emerging Technology Office

applicationRoute.detail: Connection processing and queue management for distribution
Testing on 1.5-mile distribution feeder and simulation test-bed

studies.label: EPRI Incubatenergy Labs Challenge
studies.text:
What it doesEPRI's Incubatenergy Labs connects startups directly with 50+ utility members for paid demonstration projects. Annual challenge cycle with pitch day and demo day. 2026 focus areas include AI-powered grid management, advanced data analytics, and DER integration.Why it mattersDirect pathway to paid utility demonstration contracts ($50K-$500K). International startups welcome. Southern Company, Duke Energy, PG&E and other major US utilities are members.
Incubatenergy Labs
studies.deadline: 2026 cohort closed November 2025. 2027 cycle expected to open ~September 2026. Pitch Day Spring, Demo Day Fall each year.
studies.budgetBasis: EPRI does not publish per-project budgets. Estimated from typical utility demonstration contract scales and programme scope with 50+ member utilities.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents can apply — international startups welcome. No US entity required for initial application. AI for grid connection review fits 'modernizing grids' and 'DER integration' tracks. 2027 cycle expected September 2026.

relevance.text: 2026 focus areas explicitly include AI-powered grid management and advanced data analytics. Directly connects startups to 50+ utility decision-makers for paid demonstration projects. Past cohorts included international startups. ThinkLabs AI (similar to Grid Agents) was a recent MassCEC winner.

applicationRoute.step: Pitch Day presentation to utility panel
Matched with utility partner for demonstration
applicationRoute.detail: Paid demonstration contract negotiated per project

studies.label: NYSERDA Grid Modernization Program
studies.text:
What it doesNew York State Energy Research & Development Authority funds product development and demonstration projects for grid technology startups. Partners include Con Edison (10M+ customers) and National Grid. $12M available in recent round. Previous rounds $8M-$30M each.Why it mattersDirectly funds grid technology startups with major utility partners. Explicitly seeks technologies for faster integration of renewables and managing DER impacts on distribution systems.
NYSERDA Grid Modernization
studies.deadline: Periodic rounds. April 2025 round closed July 2025. Next round expected 2026. Monitor nyserda.ny.gov.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a US entity. Partners with Con Edison and National Grid — both operate in UK and US. Grid connection queue management fits transmission & distribution reliability mandate.

relevance.text: Directly funds product development and demonstration for grid tech startups. Partners include Con Edison (10M+ customers) and National Grid (US/UK operations). $12M+ rounds with previous rounds up to $30M. Explicitly seeks technologies for faster renewable integration and DER management.

applicationRoute.step: Establish US entity if not already
Monitor NYSERDA for next open round

applicationRoute.detail: Con Edison or National Grid as demonstration site

studies.label: MassCEC InnovateMass Gridtech Spotlight
studies.text:
What it doesMassachusetts Clean Energy Center targets grid technology startups for utility demonstration partnerships with Eversource and National Grid. Up to $350K per project. Concept-paper-based entry. Recent winner ThinkLabs AI built 'AI-based digital twin for utility interconnection and DER integration' — almost identical to Grid Agents' value proposition.Why it mattersDirect validation that AI for grid interconnection is a funded use case. Partners with Eversource ($26.5B capital plan) and National Grid. Next round expected late 2026.
InnovateMass
studies.deadline: 2026 awardees announced. Next round expected late 2026. Monitor masscec.com.

eligibility.text: Grid Agents would need a US entity. Concept-paper-based entry. ThinkLabs AI (almost identical product) was a recent winner — proves the use case is funded.

relevance.text: Recent winner ThinkLabs AI built 'AI-based digital twin for utility interconnection and DER integration' — validates Grid Agents' exact use case. Partners with Eversource ($26.5B capital plan, $16.2B distribution) and National Grid. Concept-paper entry with $350K funding.

applicationRoute.step: Establish US entity if not already
Monitor MassCEC for next Gridtech Spotlight
Submit concept paper when round opens

applicationRoute.detail: With Eversource or National Grid at real sites

dsoPartners.name: Con Edison
dsoPartners.engagement: $21B grid plan. Smart grid deployment. Startup engagement primarily via NYSERDA Grid Modernization Program and EPRI Incubatenergy Labs.

dsoPartners.name: ComEd
dsoPartners.engagement: $55.9M/yr Beneficial Electrification Plan 2 pilots (2026-2028). 8 focus areas including DER integration. Actively accepting startup pilot ideas.

dsoPartners.name: Eversource
dsoPartners.engagement: $26.5B capital plan ($16.2B distribution). Startup engagement via MassCEC InnovateMass Gridtech Spotlight partnership.

dsoPartners.name: Southern Company
dsoPartners.engagement: New Ventures division + co-founder of Energy Impact Partners ($4B+ VC). Techstars Energy accelerator partner. Most engaged utility LP in EIP. Direct pilot pathway.

faq.q: Do I need a US entity to access federal funding?
faq.a: Yes, for almost all programmes. SBIR requires a US small business. DOE grants require US-based organisations. A Delaware LLC costs $1.5-3K and takes 1-2 weeks.

faq.q: What does FERC Order 2023 mean for our product?
faq.a: It's the single biggest market driver. Mandates cluster studies instead of serial processing, creating massive computational demands. Every RTO/ISO is implementing it and actively seeking automation solutions.

Extremely — roughly 3% success rate. However, the prestige and funding level ($1M-$10M) make it worth pursuing. Frame your application around the step-change in interconnection processing speed that AI enables.

faq.q: Can I partner with a National Lab without a US entity?
faq.a: Yes, CRADAs are available to international companies, though US companies have statutory preference. NREL and PNNL both have international partnership experience.

faq.q: Which RTO/ISO should I target first?
faq.a: PJM (largest US RTO, massive queue) and MISO (facing worst backlog with multi-year study delays) are the highest-priority targets. Both are actively implementing FERC Order 2023 reforms.

note: The US represents the largest market opportunity. FERC Order 2023 creates regulatory urgency. A Delaware LLC ($1.5-3K) unlocks access to the entire federal funding ecosystem including $10.5B GRIP, $4B+ SBIR, and EPRI's 50+ utility network.
